req.title: Transaction dispute (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall dispute a transaction within 60 days of posting; disputes beyond 60 days shall be rejected. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement has four testable behaviours: (1) valid dispute within 60 days succeeds with provisional credit (EP positive), (2) dispute beyond 60 days is rejected with 422 (NEG/EP negative), (3) dispute exactly on the 60-day boundary tests the limit (BVA boundary), and (4) duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key returns the original result without side-effects (STC idempotency). Together these cover all decision branches in R-FIN-084 and satisfy the minimum three-test requirement with an additional idempotency case.

tests.name: test_dispute_within_60_days_creates_dispute_and_applies_credit
preconditions: Authenticated customer token is available via <auth_fixture>
A posted transaction aged 30 days exists, returned by <transaction_factory(age_days=30)>
actions: POST /disputes with transaction_id and a unique Idempotency-Key
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_dispute_within_60_days_creates_dispute_and_applies_credit(
    api_client,          # <fixture> authenticated requests.Session
    transaction_factory, # <fixture> creates & returns posted transaction dict
):
    txn = transaction_factory(age_days=30)
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/disputes",
        json={"transaction_id": txn["id"]},
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 201, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    assert "dispute_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "open"
    assert body["provisional_credit"]["applied"] is True

tests.name: test_dispute_beyond_60_days_rejected_with_422
preconditions: Authenticated customer token is available via <auth_fixture>
A posted transaction aged 61 days exists, returned by <transaction_factory(age_days=61)>
actions: POST /disputes with transaction_id for a transaction aged 61 days
expected: Response body error code indicates dispute_window_exceeded
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_dispute_beyond_60_days_rejected_with_422(
    api_client,
    transaction_factory,
):
    txn = transaction_factory(age_days=61)
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/disputes",
        json={"transaction_id": txn["id"]},
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 422, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "dispute_window_exceeded"

tests.name: test_dispute_exactly_at_60_day_boundary_is_accepted
preconditions: Authenticated customer token is available via <auth_fixture>
A posted transaction aged exactly 60 days exists, returned by <transaction_factory(age_days=60)>
actions: POST /disputes with transaction_id for a transaction aged exactly 60 days
expected: HTTP 201 Created (boundary is inclusive per R-FIN-084: 'within 60 days')
trace: R-FIN-084 – boundary: transaction.age == 60 days (last valid day)
BVA: on-point at upper limit of acceptance window
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_dispute_exactly_at_60_day_boundary_is_accepted(
    api_client,
    transaction_factory,
):
    txn = transaction_factory(age_days=60)
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/disputes",
        json={"transaction_id": txn["id"]},
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 201, (
        f"Expected 201 at 60-day boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "dispute_id" in body
    assert body["provisional_credit"]["applied"] is True

tests.name: test_duplicate_dispute_submission_returns_original_result
preconditions: Authenticated customer token is available via <auth_fixture>
A posted transaction aged 10 days exists, returned by <transaction_factory(age_days=10)>
A fixed Idempotency-Key is chosen for both submissions
actions: POST /disputes with transaction_id and Idempotency-Key (first submission)
POST /disputes with same transaction_id and same Idempotency-Key (second submission)
expected: First call returns HTTP 201 with dispute_id
Second call returns HTTP 200 (or 201) with identical dispute_id
Second call does not create a new dispute record
provisional_credit.applied is true in both responses and credit is not double-applied
trace: R-FIN-084 – idempotency variant: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key
STC: system transitions from 'no dispute' -> 'dispute open' on first call, stays 'dispute open' on repeat call
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_duplicate_dispute_submission_returns_original_result(
    api_client,
    transaction_factory,
):
    txn = transaction_factory(age_days=10)
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"transaction_id": txn["id"]}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    first = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code == 201, first.text
    first_body = first.json()
    dispute_id = first_body["dispute_id"]

    second = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code in (200, 201), second.text
    second_body = second.json()

    assert second_body["dispute_id"] == dispute_id, (
        "Idempotent replay must return the original dispute_id"
    )
    assert second_body["provisional_credit"]["applied"] is True
